ead.archdesc:
======= RMA02585Robert T. Clausen papers34-1-2585Compiled by Jaffa PankenDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
September 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
lifesThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:38:49 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Robert T. Clausen papers
Clausen, Robert T. (Robert Theodore), 1911-1981.
34-1-2585
19.8 cubic feet. 19.8 cubic feet.
[ca. 1930-1980] Correspondence, meeting minutes, journals, field notebooks, photographs. Journals and field notes relating to Mexican trips, 1970s; correspondence relating to professional matters, including the Cornell Plantations; journals, 1950s-1970s; and miscellaneous field notes, herbarium records, and teaching records. Collection material in English 2093719
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Jaffa Panken
Date completed:
September 2003
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez and Carolyn Smith, November, 2003
Date modified:
RMC Staff, May 2013
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Biology, Cornell University.
Cornell University B.A. 1933; M.A. 1934; Ph.D. 1937.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Journals and field notes relating to Mexican trips, 1970s; correspondence relating to professional matters, including the Cornell Plantations; journals, 1950s-1970s; and miscellaneous field notes, herbarium records, and teaching records.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Notebooks from Research Trips/Journals
Series II. Correspondence
Series III. Cornell Plantations - Correspondence and Meeting Minutes
Series IV. Course Materials
Series V. Data on Finger Lakes Region Plants
Series VI. Reprints
Series VII. Field Research
Series VIII. Wiegand Herbarium
Series IX. Records
Series X. Sedum
Series XI. Plant Data
Series XII. Photos
Cite As:
Robert T. Clausen papers, #34-1-2585. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Herbaria -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Biology -- Study and teaching. Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell Plantations
2093719 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Notebooks from Research Trips/JournalsResearch trips to Mexico and the Western U.S.1976-19811
Scope and Contents
Forming the basis of Clausen's study on Sedum of the Mexican Plateau. In addition to discussing the specimens, geololgy, and vegetation of the areas canvasssed, Clausen recorded details on the logistics of the tripsField records from his research trips1940-19806
Scope and Contents
Field records from his research trips, including Glaciated Allegheny Plateau, 1975-80; Sedimentary Appalachian Mts., 1966; Central Rocky Mts., 1966, Red Mtn., Mammoth, Devils River, 1970; Cascade Mts.; Piedmont Plateau: San Bernadino Mts., 1940, 1963, 1968. Additionally, Clausen kept records of his interests in Ithaca and Cornell, such as the Herbarium; Greenhouse in Slaterville Springs; Botany Courses, 1964-5; Taxonomy Courses, 1966; and his personal possesions at Cornell, 1971.Journals, excluding major research trips1953-19704Journals - Clausen recorded items from both his personal and professional life.1960-19805Journals, trip notebooks, field notes, agenda, experiment records1949-198122Series II. PhotosPhotos of Fingerlakes Region Plants17Photos of Sedum and various other20 ======= Series I. Notebooks from Research Trips/JournalsResearch trips to Mexico and the Western U.S.1976-19811
Scope and Contents
Forming the basis of Clausen's study on Sedum of the Mexican Plateau. In addition to discussing the specimens, geololgy, and vegetation of the areas canvasssed, Clausen recorded details on the logistics of the tripsField records from his research trips1940-19806
Scope and Contents
Field records from his research trips, including Glaciated Allegheny Plateau, 1975-80; Sedimentary Appalachian Mts., 1966; Central Rocky Mts., 1966, Red Mtn., Mammoth, Devils River, 1970; Cascade Mts.; Piedmont Plateau: San Bernadino Mts., 1940, 1963, 1968. Additionally, Clausen kept records of his interests in Ithaca and Cornell, such as the Herbarium; Greenhouse in Slaterville Springs; Botany Courses, 1964-5; Taxonomy Courses, 1966; and his personal possesions at Cornell, 1971.Journals, excluding major research trips1953-19704Journals - Clausen recorded items from both his personal and professional life.1960-19805Journals, trip notebooks, field notes, agenda, experiment records1949-198122Series II.
